question: How many reactor safety valves does the NuScale RCS have?

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with overpressure protection requirements, referencing specific ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) Code cases as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a and related NRC regulatory guides. It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet regulatory conditions, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards related to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of alloys and stainless steels used, their relevant material specifications, and their compliance with ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) standards to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0022.title: Decay Heat Removal System
nuscale_ch05_0022.summary: The Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) is an engineered safety feature designed to remove residual and core decay heat from the reactor after shutdown, especially when normal cooling is unavailable, without relying on external power. It ensures the reactor coolant system temperature drops below 420°F within 36 hours, maintaining fuel and pressure boundary safety limits, and its reliability and risk significance are systematically evaluated. The DHRS operates independently of the Emergency Core Cooling System (ECCS), but both can function together if needed.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It confirms that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III, 2013 Edition standards, and classified according to NRC quality group requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance across its design power range. It outlines how steady-state parameters—such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures—are optimized for efficiency, safety margins, and electrical generation, using both software and manual calculations. The analysis also considers uncertainties in flow resistance and system design, emphasizing the plant’s low pressure losses and natural circulation capabilities.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are tested during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the procedures for valve exercising and position verification, and specifies that a one-time in-situ test of the DHRS function will be performed on the first reactor module during initial startup.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity. It interfaces with several other systems, including chemical control, emergency cooling, and control rod drives.

Section A provides a general system description of the RCS and mentions reactor vent valves (RVVs) as part of ECCS operation, while Section B specifies the detailed design and materials of pressurizer components, including references to other sections for further detail.

Section A discusses system evaluation criteria for the NuScale Power Plant, while Section B describes specific components of the RCS, such as reactor vent valves (RVVs), but neither section provides the number of reactor safety valves, with Section B offering more relevant component details that could support answering the analysis question.

Section B describes the presence and function of reactor vent valves (RVVs) in the NuScale RCS, while Section A discusses inspection requirements for actuation valves but does not specify the number of safety valves, thus Section A provides supporting detail about valve types and regulatory classification relevant to the valves mentioned in Section B.
